item.title: An important message for men to hear...
item.description: "I'd rather me mate cry on me shoulder than go to his funeral next week." - Paddy Pimblett SMS 988 in the USA for Text Suicide Prevention Support
item.dc:creator: IamJACKSventedSPLEEN

item.title: It be hot as hec
item.description:
I wasn't made for this past weeks highest of 40 degrees Celsius, it's 30 today and I think I'm melting into a puddle :'D My favourite boots are in storage until autumn, so let me introduce you to my summer footwear; peach chunky sneakers!
Cats tax: Mr. Chonk and the Bean! Despite wearing fur coats they don't seem even the slightest bit bothered by the heat! If you would like to see more of my illustrations you can find them on my Instagram (I don't post them here as much as I do there): https://www.instagram.com/revives_illustrations
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.dc:creator: cinnabarra

item.title: I built a thing
item.description:
Obligatory finished photo first. I've always wanted to build a secret bookcase door, and after finally moving out of rentals, I had the opportunity to test my diy skills on a childhood dream.
Finished in action, finally!
After lots of YouTube videos for inspiration, and careful design in sketch up, all that was left to do was start drilling holes in my new house. It was really hard to drill a clean hole in the concrete lintel above the doorway, so I used construction adhesive and some fabricated brackets to fix the top brace in place. You can see the hinge that I bought off Amazon on the right. It has a grub screw that retracts the hinge pin so you can install the door, I had to route it into the wood to house the mechanism.
This is the bottom component for the hinge, a ball bearing sits in the top of the pin and slips into the female side of the hinge plate on the bottom of the door.
Time to make the base of the bookcase. I thought two castor wheels would help with the weight distribution, but it turned out that only one maintained contact with the floor when opening. These wheels were too hard as well and made a lot of noise as they bumped across the texture of the flooring, but more on that later. You can see the female side of the hinge here as well, recessed into the wooden foot of the base.
Went to bunnings and picked up a 1200x2400mm (roughly 8x10 bananas) sheet of 19mm plywood and had it cut down into planks in the cut shop there.
It's dado time! I didn't have a router, so tried to cut dados with my circular saw with limited success. Look at that monster. Took pause and borrowed a friends router and was able to rescue this monstrosity.
Here we are, doing it properly with a jig I made out of some offcuts and an old block mount print.
Testing the fit, snug af.
Gotta get the kids involved! My daughter helping me recess the screw holes during assembly.
First of several moments of truth, measure twice (at least) and hopefully cut once! The lip at the top is to attach the female side of the top hinge. I left a bit of space on both sides for any future mechanisms, or things I hadn't considered.
We have a skeleton! Spooky! Assembly of the main frame on the shop floor.
First actual test of the fit, and door travel on the hinges, this was real exciting.
Headed back to bunnings for a sheet of 5mm plywood and attached it to the back. Starting to look like a bookcase now.
Time to make the opening mechanism.
Here it is in action. I pushed some aluminium tube through the side of the bookcase, which some threaded bar goes through, attaching to the "book", and the opening mechanism. As the book is pulled forward it retracts the steel bar down out of a hole drilled in the top timber brace.
I didn't want the book to stay open while the door was open, but also, I didn't want the steel bar to be up while the door was open because if it was closed, the bar would damage the top of the frame. So I routed the inside of the book out so the book could be returned to the shelf without actuating the door mechanism. This meant that I needed another system to lock the door when it was closed. I ended up using some levers and springs to accomplish this.
Here are the levers, the spring came a bit later. I didn't plan this part from the start of the build, so I had to weld the extension onto the original part. I installed a wood block to limit the movement.
I decided that the original castors needed to go. So I fabricated this little bracket for my soft skateboard wheel. I left a tail of the back of the bracket so I could easily adjust the angle to fine tune the travel across the floor. It's not pretty, but it works well.
Starting to add the finishing trim now. I bought a brad nailer for this job, couldn't have done without it! The left side of the trim is fixed to the wall (not yet though), and the right side is attached to the frame so that the door is able to be opened.
Here the left trim is attached to the wall, I used construction adhesive and brad nails to fix it to the plaster.
Lots of sanding, and planing, and fine tuning to minimise any gaps. I felt that If you can clearly see its a door, I failed, so this step was crucial
Final result, I'm happy with that gap
Time to make a handle for the inside, I just fabricated this out of some 40mm steel tubing welded together with miter joints.
Ready to attach! I'm not a good welder, but I'm a good grinder!
Attached to the door, you can see the spring on the closer now, and I replaced the wood stops with nylon bushings.
Painting day! (I hate painting)
Finished!
item.dc:creator: Fergaperson

item.title: The Leopards Ate My Face Party
item.description:
Trump man is shocked when wife is deported.
Trump woman shocked when her husband is deported. These are the "very good people" who make up the Trump party. They're the sort to vote against their loved ones. We have no hope of swaying most of them, but you can vote. You can vote in favor of your mom, your wife, your, sister, your daughter. In favor of your lgbtq+ family and friends. To vote in favor of compassion and humanity.
item.dc:creator: TheRealSleepIsForChumps

item.title: Hasta La Vista...
item.dc:creator: sleeperkid

item.title: The Best News of Last Week
item.description:
1. Stray Is Raising Money for Homeless Cats with Game Giveaway - Annapurna, the publisher of the game Stray, has teamed up with a few cat shelters and humane societies to help them raise funds by giving away copies of Stray for PC, PS4, and PS5. These are Cats Protection, a UK-based cat welfare charity, as well as Nebraska Humane Cats and Nebraska Humane Society. https://twitter.com/CatsProtection/status/1547966845109448704
2. Five ways the world is actually doing better – in data - • Child mortality is at an all-time low. • Childbirth is much safer. • Life expectancy more than doubled in a century. • Natural disasters are getting less deadly. • Extreme poverty is at all time low level ( https://www.euronews.com/next/2022/07/17/is-humanity-doomed-five-ways-the-world-is-actually-doing-better-in-data )
3. EA Refuses to Switch LGBTQIA+ Orientation Identities in Sims - EA has featured representations of the LGBTQ community by allowing the users to control their Sims sexual orientations. A bunch of players of Sims 4 asked for a toggle to turn off the new sexual orientations. However, EA denied it. Electronic Arts, the game designer company, said no to adding the toggle to switch sexual orientation.
4. A YouTuber has gone to an unclaimed baggage store to purchase lost items and send them back to their owners. ( https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v5aHcu19wio )
5. Kākāpō population at its highest in almost 50 years - Kākāpō conservation efforts are paying dividends with the population reaching its highest level since records began in the 1970s. As of today, the New Zealand Dept of Conservation site shows a population of 197. All known kakapo reside on four predator free islands Whenua Hou, Pukenui, Hauturu, and Te Kakahu.
6. Botswana on path to seeing no babies born with HIV - Country may be Africa’s first to stop mother-baby transmission as WHO hails ‘groundbreaking’ fall in rates from 40% to 1%.
7. Nine lions rescued from Ukraine to be given ‘heaven’ at US sanctuary - A pride of nine lions is due to be flown 14,000 miles to the US after being rescued from Ukraine in a non-stop drive across three countries. The big cats were transported in a convoy from Odessa in the south of the war zone and driven to a temporary home in Romania.
That's it for this week :) Do you want more good news? https://feelgoodnews.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: FeelGoodNews

The meme I came here to post. Positive thoughts are a gift you can give yourself.

item.title: I would also like to boop all the murder floofs
item.description: Okay, I found this cute and shared. But as posted by few people below, there's a high probability of exploitation of these animals. link of a source : https://www.reddit.com/r/forbiddenboops/comments/taanh0/seen_way_too_many_of_safarisammies_videos I am not deleting this post but removing link to their channel and adding this description to let people know.
